pi_items.source: Web search confirmed Jantar Mantar is a real observatory in Jaipur, India.
Web search confirmed latitude of Jantar Mantar is approx 26.9247. Matches task description.
Web search confirmed longitude of Jantar Mantar is approx 75.8244. Matches task description.
Calculated from 26.9246 degrees (26.9246 * pi / 180 = 0.4699 rad). Matches verifier.py JAIPUR_LAT_RAD.
Calculated from 75.8235 degrees (75.8235 * pi / 180 = 1.3233 rad). Matches verifier.py JAIPUR_LON_RAD.
Web search confirmed elevation of Jantar Mantar is approx 430m. Matches task description.
Explicitly defined in the task description for the 2023 equinox/solstice dates at 06:30 UTC (12:00 IST).
Explicitly required by the task description and checked in export_result.sh and verifier.py.
Explicitly defined in the task description and checked by export_result.sh and verifier.py.
Explicitly defined in the task description and checked by setup_task.sh and export_result.sh.
Explicitly defined in the task description and verifier.py (new_ss >= 4).

privileged_info_summary: The task requires setting Stellarium to the Jantar Mantar observatory in Jaipur, India. Web searches confirmed this is a real historical site, with the provided coordinates (26.9246° N, 75.8235° E) and altitude (~431m) matching real-world data. These coordinates convert to 0.4699 and 1.3233 radians respectively, matching the values hardcoded in the verifier.py. The task's required 2023 equinox and solstice dates (March 20, June 21, September 23, December 22) and the specific time of 06:30 UTC (corresponding to 12:00 IST noon) are explicitly confirmed from the task instructions. The requirements for Stellarium display flags (atmosphere on, landscape off, azimuthal grid on, cardinal points on) and the expected output paths (/home/ga/Desktop/sundial_reference.txt and /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium) have been verified by cross-referencing the task description with export_result.sh and verifier.py.
